compiled_state.title: As a DSO user, bugs I ask the agent to file are automatically tagged CLI_user
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add guidance in CLAUDE.md and agent instruction files so the agent applies the CLI_user tag when creating bugs on behalf of the user during interactive conversation, and does NOT apply it for autonomously-discovered bugs.
**Why**: The tag must be applied at the right moment (user-requested bug creation) and NOT applied in the wrong context (sub-agent discovered defects). This is a behavioral directive enforced via instruction, consistent with how other agent behavioral rules work in this system.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md guidance for CLI_user tag application, sub-agent boundaries update clarifying that autonomous bug creation must NOT use CLI_user, fix-bug SKILL.md Step 2.85 (discovered bugs) clarification
- OUT: The --tags flag implementation (story 1), the Gate 1a skip logic (story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md contains guidance that when the user explicitly asks for a bug to be created, the agent includes --tags CLI_user in the ticket create command
  ← Satisfies: "When the agent creates a bug ticket on behalf of the user during interactive conversation, the bug is tagged CLI_user"
- When this story is complete, sub-agent boundary documentation explicitly states that autonomously-discovered bugs must NOT carry the CLI_user tag
  ← Satisfies: "Bugs created autonomously by sub-agents do NOT carry the CLI_user tag"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] CLAUDE.md is a safeguard file (Never-Do rule 18) — edits require user approval during sprint execution
